categories.label: Personal life
questions: What should people understand about you before they ask for advice from your model?
What parts of your life should be treated with extra care when someone asks about you?
How should your model speak when a question touches your personal story?
What should your model never guess about your private life?
What does a respectful answer about you sound like?

categories.label: Family life
questions: How should your model protect family matters when someone asks about them?
What family topics should stay private unless you clearly approve them?
How should your model respond when a family question needs warmth but also boundaries?
What should your model avoid saying about people close to you?
How should your model handle questions that could affect your family relationships?

categories.label: Relationship logic
questions: How should your model respond when someone asks about trust, loyalty, or commitment?
What should your model do when a relationship question needs emotional care?
How should your model balance honesty and kindness in personal relationship advice?
What boundaries should your model keep when someone asks about your relationships?
How should your model respond when someone asks for advice about conflict?

categories.label: Friendship logic
questions: How should your model recognize a respectful friendship question?
What should your model reveal to friends that it would not reveal publicly?
How should your model handle humor, memory, and privacy with friends?
What should your model do when a friend asks something emotionally sensitive?
How should your model respond when friendship and privacy point in different directions?

categories.label: Education logic
questions: How should your model explain something when a person is learning for the first time?
What kind of teaching style should your model use when a topic is difficult?
How should your model correct a misunderstanding without sounding dismissive?
When should your model simplify an answer, and when should it go deeper?
How should your model help someone learn without pretending to know more than it does?

categories.label: Business logic
questions: How should your model respond when a business question needs strategic judgment?
What business information should your model keep private by default?
How should your model handle questions about risk, money, or responsibility?
What should your model do when a business answer needs evidence before confidence?
How should your model balance commercial clarity with your values?

categories.label: Values and boundaries
questions: What values should guide your model when there is no obvious right answer?
What should your model refuse to do even if someone asks politely?
How should your model decide between being helpful and protecting your boundaries?
What should your model do when a question could misrepresent you?
How should your model speak when it needs to say no?

categories.label: Decision making
questions: How should your model make a decision when the facts are incomplete?
When should your model ask a clarifying question before answering?
How should your model weigh truth, speed, privacy, and care when they conflict?
What sources should your model trust first when memories and documents disagree?
How should your model show uncertainty without becoming vague?
